---
title: "Veille arXiv"
date: today
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD"
---

# Veille arXiv

## Catégories surveillées

| Catégorie arXiv | Description | Fréquence de scan |
|-----------------|-------------|-------------------|
| `cs.AI` | Artificial Intelligence | Quotidien |
| `cs.CL` | Computation and Language (NLP) | Quotidien |
| `cs.LG` | Machine Learning | Quotidien |
| `cs.MA` | Multi-Agent Systems | Quotidien |
| `cs.SE` | Software Engineering | Quotidien |
| `stat.ML` | Machine Learning (Stats) | Quotidien |

## Filtres de pertinence

Le script `scripts/veille_arxiv.py` filtre les papers selon :

| Filtre | Mots-clés | Action |
|--------|-----------|--------|
| Agentic | `agent`, `agentic`, `autonomous`, `tool-use` | Priorité haute |
| LLM | `LLM`, `language model`, `transformer`, `GPT`, `Claude` | Priorité haute |
| Sandbox | `sandbox`, `isolation`, `container`, `microVM` | Priorité moyenne |
| RAG | `RAG`, `retrieval augmented`, `GraphRAG`, `knowledge graph` | Priorité haute |
| MCP | `MCP`, `model context protocol` | Priorité haute |
| Harness | `code agent`, `IDE`, `coding assistant` | Priorité moyenne |
| Benchmark | `benchmark`, `evaluation`, `leaderboard` | Priorité moyenne |

## Format de sortie

Chaque paper détecté génère une entrée dans `content/veille/arxiv-feed.qmd` :

```markdown
### [Titre du paper]
- **arXiv** : 2507.17311
- **Date** : 2025-07-23
- **Auteurs** : Intern-AI et al.
- **Catégorie** : cs.AI
- **Tags** : agentic, climate, research
- **Résumé** : Premier self-evolving AI agent pour climate science...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2507.17311
```

## Derniers papers détectés

::: callout-note
## Section auto-générée
Cette section est automatiquement mise à jour par le script `scripts/veille_arxiv.py` via GitHub Actions. Les entrées ci-dessous sont générées à chaque exécution.
:::

<!-- ARXIV_FEED_START -->
### RAGU: A Multi-Step GraphRAG Engine with a Compact Domain-Adapted LLM
- **arXiv** : 2607.11683v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-13
- **Auteurs** : Mikhail Komarov, Ivan Bondarenko, Stanislav Shtuka, Oleg Sedukhin, Roman Shuvalov
- **Score** : 15
- **Résumé** : Graph retrieval-augmented generation (GraphRAG) enhances large language models with structured knowledge, yet existing systems construct knowledge graphs in a single extraction pass, producing noisy entities and brittle retrieval. RAGU, an open-source modular GraphRAG engine, addresses this by separ...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.11683v1

### How Temperature Shapes Ideological Discourse in Retrieval-Augmented Generation?
- **arXiv** : 2607.11783v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-13
- **Auteurs** : Elmira Salari, Hazem Amamou, José Victor de Souza, Shruti Kshirsagar, Maria Nunes Delfino
- **Score** : 10
- **Résumé** : Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) has been increasingly adopted to reduce hallucinations and strengthen the factual grounding of large language models (LLMs). While robustness to errors in the retrieval process has been explored, the impact of ideological bias on LLM outputs has been overlooked. ...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.11783v1

### AdvancedMathBench: A Benchmark Suite for Advanced Mathematical Proof Generation and Verification
- **arXiv** : 2607.11849v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-13
- **Auteurs** : Lingkai Kong, Zijian Wu, Yuzhe Gu, Haiteng Zhao, Wenyong Huang
- **Score** : 8
- **Résumé** : Large language models (LLMs) have achieved remarkable performance on high-school and olympiad-style mathematics, yet their capabilities on advanced mathematics remain poorly understood. Existing benchmarks, however, fall short in both scope and evaluation granularity: they provide limited disciplina...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.11849v1

### MM-ToolSandBox: A Unified Framework for Evaluating Visual Tool-Calling Agents
- **arXiv** : 2607.11818v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-13
- **Auteurs** : Kaixin Ma, Di Feng, Alexander Metz, Jiarui Lu, Eshan Verma
- **Score** : 8
- **Résumé** : We introduce MM-ToolSandBox, a benchmark and evaluation framework for visually grounded tool-calling agents. The framework provides a stateful execution environment spanning 500+ tools across 16 application domains, supporting multi-image, multi-turn tasks where agents must ground progressively arri...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.11818v1

### Agent Hacks Agent: Autoresearch for Production-Agent Red-Teaming
- **arXiv** : 2607.11698v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-13
- **Auteurs** : Xutao Mao, Xiang Zheng, Cong Wang
- **Score** : 7
- **Résumé** : Production LLM agents such as Claude Code and Codex operate over untrusted content, files, commands, and workspace state, making safety failures directly actionable. Red-teaming must therefore keep pace with evolving models and tools. Existing approaches mainly optimize attack success and preserve a...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.11698v1

### Evidence-Backed Video Question Answering
- **arXiv** : 2607.11862v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-13
- **Auteurs** : Shijie Wang, Honglu Zhou, Ziyang Wang, Ran Xu, Caiming Xiong
- **Score** : 6
- **Résumé** : Current Video Large Language Models (Video LLMs) excel in question answering (QA) but largely operate as black boxes, providing textual answers without verifiable visual grounding. Existing explainability efforts rely on textual rationales or sparse bounding boxes, which struggle to capture complex ...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.11862v1

### Can LLMs Perform Deep Technical Comprehension of Computer Architecture Papers?
- **arXiv** : 2607.11859v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-13
- **Auteurs** : Nishant Aggarwal, Ayushi Dubal, Sreeraj Kannakarankodi, Ian McDougall, Adarsh Mittal
- **Score** : 6
- **Résumé** : Can large language models perform deep technical comprehension of computer architecture papers -- not summarization, but structured critique that names the core mechanism, surfaces buried assumptions, and connects a contribution beyond its own scope? We study Gauntlet, an open-source pipeline that a...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.11859v1

### From Expressivity to Sample Complexity: Narrow Teachers for Transformers via C-RASP
- **arXiv** : 2607.11760v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-13
- **Auteurs** : Michael Rizvi-Martel, Satwik Bhattamishra, Guillaume Rabusseau, Michael Hahn
- **Score** : 6
- **Résumé** : A theoretical understanding of Transformers is crucial to better understand the capacities and limitations of large language models (LLMs). There is much work analyzing the expressivity of attention-based models. By proposing handcrafted weights or using computational complexity arguments, a large a...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.11760v1

### When Local Monitors Miss Compositional Harm: Diagnosing Distributed Backdoors in Multi-Agent Systems
- **arXiv** : 2607.11751v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-13
- **Auteurs** : Yibo Hu, Ren Wang
- **Score** : 6
- **Résumé** : As multi-agent, tool-using LLM systems are deployed, a common safety net is a runtime monitor that checks each message, tool call, or step on its own. We show this net has a fundamental hole. A distributed backdoor splits a harmful payload across agents, so every local check passes while the assembl...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.11751v1

### Playful AI in Professional Email: A Field Experiment on Tone and Recipient Engagement
- **arXiv** : 2607.11749v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-13
- **Auteurs** : Ziv Ben-Zion, Teddy Lazebnik
- **Score** : 6
- **Résumé** : Large language models (LLMs) are rapidly reshaping workplace communication, yet whether AI-assisted writing changes how recipients actually behave, and through what channel, remains unknown. Here, in a randomized crossover field experiment, 121 employees across six companies sent work emails under t...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.11749v1

### MET: Theory-Grounded and Culture-Aware Multilingual Moral Reasoning
- **arXiv** : 2607.11736v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-13
- **Auteurs** : Ayoung Lee, Ryan Kwon, Yunxiang Zhang, Yuxuan Liu, Peter Railton
- **Score** : 6
- **Résumé** : Language models are increasingly used for moral decision-making across diverse linguistic and cultural contexts, yet existing work overlooks multilinguality on three aspects: 1) multilingual evaluation benchmarks use direct translation, failing to adapt culture-specific items; 2) inference-time meth...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.11736v1

### An Explainable Agentic System for Detection of Conversational Scams with Summary-Based Memory
- **arXiv** : 2607.11707v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-13
- **Auteurs** : Ahmed Omar Salim Adnan, Yogananda Manjunath, Shivanjali Khare
- **Score** : 6
- **Résumé** : Following the rapid progress of generative Artificial Intelligence, there is a growing threat posed by conversational scams. These scams often span over multiple weeks or months, gradually build trust and request for money or sensitive information. Existing scam-detection systems mainly focus on iso...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.11707v1

### Production and Perception in LLMs: A Token Probability Approach
- **arXiv** : 2607.11703v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-13
- **Auteurs** : Anna Marklová, Jiří Milička, Martina Vokáčová, Rudolf Rosa
- **Score** : 6
- **Résumé** : The asymmetry between language production and perception has been well-documented in psycholinguistics. Whether large language models (LLMs) exhibit a functionally analogous distinction remains an open question, particularly given that LLMs rely on the same underlying mechanism (next-token predictio...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.11703v1

### Inside the Unfair Judge: A Mechanistic Interpretability Account of LLM-as-Judge Bias
- **arXiv** : 2607.11871v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-13
- **Auteurs** : Zixiang Xu, Sixian Li, Huaxing Liu, Xiang Wang, Shuai Li
- **Score** : 4
- **Résumé** : Existing studies of LLM-as-judge scoring bias work predominantly at the input-output level: they perturb inputs, measure score deltas, and propose prompt-level mitigations. We argue that the same biases admit a representation-level account in the judge's hidden state, complementary to the input-outp...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.11871v1

### Encoder-Side Neuron Identification and Amplification for Acoustic Perception in Large Audio-Language Models
- **arXiv** : 2607.11801v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-13
- **Auteurs** : Yu-Han Huang, Chih-Kai Yang, Ke-Han Lu, An-Yu Cheng, Hung-yi Lee
- **Score** : 4
- **Résumé** : Large audio-language models (LALMs) often underperform on fine-grained, non-semantic attributes of speech, such as a speaker's emotion, despite strong performance on speech content. Improving this without the cost of retraining calls for an effective inference-time intervention, yet most existing me...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.11801v1

### VoxENES 2026: Benchmarking Generalization of Speech Spoofing Detectors Against LLM-Era TTS and Voice Conversion
- **arXiv** : 2607.11706v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-13
- **Auteurs** : Aastha Sharma, Guangjing Wang
- **Score** : 4
- **Résumé** : Modern LLM-driven text-to-speech (TTS) and voice conversion (VC) systems produce synthetic speech that differs from the generators represented in many legacy spoofing benchmarks. This mismatch creates a temporal generalization gap that can overestimate detector robustness under real-world post-proce...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.11706v1

### Metacognition in LLMs: Foundations, Progress, and Opportunities
- **arXiv** : 2607.11881v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-13
- **Auteurs** : Gabrielle Kaili-May Liu, Areeb Gani, Jacqueline Lu, Jordan Thomas, Mark Steyvers
- **Score** : 3
- **Résumé** : Metacognition is a foundational component of intelligence critical to effective learning, problem solving, decision-making, communication, and more. In recent years, it has become increasingly recognized as a cornerstone of capable, transparent AI systems. Yet while LLMs have made significant progre...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.11881v1

### Invariant Learning Dynamics of Transformers in Inductive Reasoning Tasks
- **arXiv** : 2607.11875v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-13
- **Auteurs** : Tiberiu Musat, Tiago Pimentel, Nicholas Zucchet, Thomas Hofmann
- **Score** : 3
- **Résumé** : We present a theoretical framework to explain the emergence of inductive reasoning abilities in Transformer language models. While previous works on Transformer learning dynamics have so far been mostly tied to specific tasks, we study a generalized class of inductive tasks that unifies several synt...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.11875v1

### StoryTeller: Training-Free Narrative Grounding for Long-Form Audio Description
- **arXiv** : 2607.11798v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-13
- **Auteurs** : Seung Hyun Hahm, Minh T. Dinh, SouYoung Jin
- **Score** : 3
- **Résumé** : Long-form audio description (AD) requires more than describing visible actions: it must preserve characters, events, relationships, and story context across scenes so that blind and low-vision (BLV) audiences can follow a film. Modern video-language models (VLMs) are effective on short clips, but th...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.11798v1

### Forgetting Our Way to Shared Meaning: Effects of Forgetting on Conceptual Alignment in a Non-Partnership Coordination Game
- **arXiv** : 2607.11787v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-13
- **Auteurs** : Landon Liu, Mary Kelly, Alan Tsang
- **Score** : 3
- **Résumé** : Shared meaning in language requires people to learn and agree on categories. We ask how characteristics of agents' memories change the emergence and evolution of shared meaning. Without a coordination game, models of conceptual semantics cannot explain how shared meaning emerges and changes in group...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.11787v1

<!-- ARXIV_FEED_END -->
